description: The source-return / RMA record (SCOR S4.1-S4.5) for goods already received from a supplier: initiate, authorize (supplier RMA number), identify condition/reason, schedule the return shipment, and close or adjust with the matching credit note (supplier fault) or debit note (buyer recovery), reversing the GR/IR accrual and reconciling the commitment for the returned quantity. Entered either from an NCR disposition = return (carrying linkedNcrId) or buyer-initiated (over-delivery, wrong item, supplier-acknowledged error, linkedNcrId null). No goods ship before an authorized RMA number exists.

fields.name: rmaNumber
fields.label: RMA No.
fields.validation: RET/##### pattern (configurable); unique; immutable for the life of the return
fields.purpose: Stable, immutable return identifier that links the return to its source PO, NCR and credit/debit note end to end; the handle the supplier, ledger and scorecard all reference (the supplier-issued authorization reference is held separately in supplierRmaNumber).

fields.name: sourceOrderId
fields.label: Source Purchase Order
fields.validation: resolves to the PO header the goods were received against; not null
fields.purpose: Anchors the return to the order it reverses, so the commitment for the returned value can be reconciled and the GR/IR accrual reversed; the order the supplier and buyer dispute against (S4.1).

fields.name: supplierId
fields.label: Supplier
fields.purpose: Names the supplier the goods return to; the return event feeds the supplier scorecard quality and four-factor perfect-order delivery dimensions on closure.

fields.name: linkedNcrId
fields.label: Linked NCR
fields.mandatoryCondition: mandatory if the return was triggered by an NCR disposition = return; null for a buyer-initiated return
fields.validation: must resolve to an existing NCR when set; null permitted for buyer-initiated returns
fields.purpose: Ties an NCR-triggered return back to its non-conformance so closure marks the NCR disposition executed and hands systemic root-cause to the CAPA loop; null marks the buyer-initiated entry (over-delivery / wrong item) outside the NCR path (axiom A11).

fields.name: reason
fields.label: Return Reason
fields.validation: one of the enum; drives the financial direction at closure
fields.purpose: Classifies why the goods are returned (defective/damaged/wrong-item/over-delivery/expired/quality-fail); supplier-fault reasons expect a credit note, buyer-recovery reasons (over-delivery/wrong-item) raise a debit note (S4.3).

fields.name: authorizationStatus
fields.label: Authorization Status
fields.validation: authorized with an rmaNumber proceeds; declined or still requested after the reminder SLA escalates to re-disposition (e04); no goods move before authorized
fields.purpose: The supplier authorization gate (S4.2): only authorized lets the physical return ship; declined sets the return blocked and routes back to e04 for an alternative disposition, where a unilateral debit note may still be raised.

fields.name: supplierRmaNumber
fields.label: Supplier RMA Reference
fields.validation: non-empty before the S4.4 shipment step
fields.purpose: The supplier-issued authorization reference recorded via the email+OTP external form; no goods are dispatched until it is set, the hard gate on the S4.4 shipment step.

fields.name: quantityToReturn
fields.label: Quantity to Return
fields.validation: > 0 AND <= received quantity for the line; the credit/debit and GR/IR reversal are pro-rata to this
fields.purpose: The quantity being sent back; bounds a partial return and sizes the pro-rata credit/debit note, GR/IR reversal and commitment reconciliation for the returned portion (S4.1).

fields.name: productCondition
fields.label: Product Condition
fields.validation: non-empty; max 2000 chars; classifies the condition behind the reason
fields.purpose: Describes the physical/functional condition of the returned goods (with percentNonConformance copied from the linked NCR if present); the S4.3 condition record the supplier needs to accept the return.

fields.name: carrier
fields.label: Return Carrier
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the return shipment is scheduled (S4.4)
fields.validation: from carrier master or free text
fields.purpose: The carrier moving the goods back to the supplier; the manual tracking handle for the return leg (S4.4), mirroring the inbound tracking on the original consignment.

fields.name: returnLabelRef
fields.label: Return Label / Who Pays
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the return shipment is scheduled (S4.4)
fields.validation: return label reference / attachment; who-pays in {supplier-paid, buyer-paid, shared}, default supplier-paid for defective/quality-fail
fields.purpose: The return shipping label and the who-pays-freight decision; supplier-paid freight defaults for supplier-fault reasons, and buyer-paid freight may be added to a debit note (S4.4).

fields.name: returnTrackingNumber
fields.label: Return Tracking No.
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the return shipment is dispatched (S4.4)
fields.validation: AWB / BL / LR / courier number per mode
fields.purpose: The transport-document reference for the return leg; the field a handler watches to confirm the goods reach the supplier and to fire the ETA-approaching alarm (S4.4).

fields.name: returnIncoterm
fields.label: Return Incoterm
fields.mandatoryCondition: set for a cross-border return shipment
fields.validation: Incoterms 2020; validate against transport mode (FOB/CIF/CFR sea-only; air/multimodal use FCA/CPT/CIP/DAP)
fields.purpose: Sets the delivery terms for a cross-border return so freight and customs responsibility are clear; validated against transport mode for consistency on the return leg (S4.4).

fields.name: shipmentDate
fields.label: Shipment / Pickup Date
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the return shipment is scheduled (S4.4)
fields.purpose: The pickup/dispatch date for the return; sets the return status to in-transit, arms the ETA-approaching alarm, and dates the return leg for tracking (S4.4).

fields.name: segregationLocation
fields.label: Segregation Location
fields.mandatoryCondition: set while goods are quarantined awaiting dispatch
fields.purpose: Records where the nonconforming goods are physically segregated (quarantined) from raising until dispatch, the ISO 8.7 segregation control on the returned lot.

fields.name: creditDebitNoteId
fields.label: Credit / Debit Note
fields.mandatoryCondition: set at closure when a financial adjustment is raised (Branch A credit note / Branch B debit note); null for accrual-only reversal (Branch C, goods not yet invoiced)
fields.validation: resolves to a CreditNote (supplier fault) or DebitNote (buyer recovery) linked to this return
fields.purpose: Links the return to the note that adjusts the payable: a supplier credit note reduces the payable for supplier-fault reasons, a buyer debit note recovers over-billed/disputed value; null when nothing was invoiced and only the GR/IR accrual is reversed (S4.5).

fields.name: closureStatus
fields.label: Closure Status
fields.validation: raised on initiate; authorized on RMA; blocked on decline; in-transit on dispatch; closed when goods returned/written off, payable adjusted, GR/IR reversed and NCR/scorecard fed (S4.5)
fields.purpose: The return lifecycle position; closure always reverses the GR/IR accrual, reconciles the commitment for the returned quantity, updates the linked NCR and feeds the supplier scorecard, and an open return is a block on requisition COMPLETED (axiom A3).

invariants: A3 an open return blocks the requisition reaching COMPLETED
A11 a return disposition is part of the NCR/CAPA loop and feeds re-evaluation
A12 returned-value amounts normalized to base via FX; tax reversed on the returned portion
A13 audit + SSE on every return change
no physical return ships before an authorized supplier RMA number exists (S4.2)
closure always reverses GR/IR and reconciles the commitment for the returned quantity (S4.5)
incoterm/transport-mode consistency on the return leg (FOB/CIF/CFR sea-only)
